text: ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 Al-Akhbar: Israeli forces have destroyed the ancient fortress and shrine of Simon al-Safa in the town of Shama, southern Lebanon, using ground bulldozers and airstrikes. The site, located about 5 kilometers from the Israeli border, is traditionally associated with St. Peter. Israeli Army Radio reports that it was the scene of a clash about a year and a half ago in which Israeli researcher Zeev Erlich and Golani soldier Gur Kehati were killed. @wfwitness

✌️ 🇮🇶 Hamas leadership council head Muhammad Darwish congratulated Iraqi President Nizar Muhammad Sa'id al-Amidi on his election, wishing him success. He affirmed that al-Amidi's election represents an important step toward strengthening stability and national unity in Iraq, reflecting the great confidence he enjoys. Darwish emphasized Hamas's pride in the fraternal relations and historical solidarity between the Palestinian and Iraqi peoples, stressing the importance of continued Iraqi support for the Palestinian cause to achieve freedom, independence, and return.

OSINTdefender RT @DefenseBulletin : Keeping this in mind, it’s now just under two hours before the U.S. blockade of vessels leaving Iranian ports and vessels paying tolls to Iran is set to begin. According to Barak Ravid, citing a senior U.S. official, following the failed U.S.-Iran talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, mediators from Turkey, Pakistan, And Egypt will continue talks with their U.S. counterparts in an attempt to find points of convergence between the U.S. and Iranian sides. Per the report, mediators, who still believe a lasting ceasefire is possible, hope to realize the aforementioned goals before the ceasefire deadline on April 12, 2026. - OSINTdefender tweet

In a separate incident, the Maglan commando unit identified three fighters, who were killed in a precision airstrike. The IDF also destroyed a loaded launcher ready to fire at Israel. @wfwitness
